items.title: Customs seizes frozen chicken, pork worth RM2.23 million at Port Klang
items.summary: KLANG: The Customs Department has seized four containers of frozen chicken and pork worth RM2.23 million, including duties and taxes, at the North Port Free Zone in Port Klang. He said enforcement officers inspected four 40-foot refrigerated containers and discovered 112,880kg of frozen meat believed to have been imported without valid permits. "Three of the containers contained 84,000kg of frozen chicken, estimated to be worth RM1.09 million, with duties and taxes amounting to RM218,400.

items.title: Jaywalking Singaporean hit by e-hailing car in JB
items.summary: Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat said the accident occurred at 3.40pm on April 3 at Km 2, Jalan Tun Abdul Razak. Preliminary investigations found that a Malaysian e-hailing driver was travelling along the stretch when the Singaporean suddenly dashed across the road. "The driver was unable to avoid the pedestrian," he said in a statement.

items.title: Ex-sales executive pleads not guilty to over RM1mil fraud charges
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A former sales executive of a housing company pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court on Tuesday to five counts of criminal breach of trust involving more than RM1 million. Rafidah Abdul Hamid, 48, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read before Sessions Court judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. The accused is alleged to have misappropriated company funds ranging from RM73,000 to RM317,610 for her personal use in each of the five charges, totalling over RM1 million.

items.title: Australian PM to discuss fuel supplies in Singapore visit
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia's Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Tuesday he will fly to Singapore to help secure petroleum imports as oil prices surge during war in the Middle East. Albanese said he would travel to Singapore from April 9-11 to discuss trade in "essential supplies" such as diesel and liquefied natural gas with Prime Minister Lawrence Wong. Service station outages of diesel and unleaded gasoline in Australia are falling, the government said.

items.title: Man killed in parang attack in Buntong, several injured
items.summary: IPOH: A 25-year-old man was killed and several others injured in a parang attack in Buntong last night, police said. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Muhammad Najib Hamzah said a man has been arrested, while police continue efforts to track down other suspects. Police have urged anyone with information to contact the nearest police station or Ipoh district headquarters.

items.title: Anwar to urge Asean, China to fast-track South China Sea Code of Conduct at May summit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said he will urge his regional counterparts and China to expedite the conclusion of Code of Conduct (COC) on the South China Sea at the Asean Leaders’ Summit in Philippines next month. During the 2025 Asean Summit in Kuala Lumpur last October, Anwar said Chinese Premier Li Qiang has agreed to expedite talks on the COC. Asean and China signed the non-binding Declaration on the Conduct of Asean and China (DOC) in 2002 and initiated talks for a legally-binding COC in 2013.

items.title: Mindef: Defence projects, including Navy LCS, to proceed as scheduled
items.summary: LUMUT: The Defence Ministry will continue all major projects as scheduled, including the Royal Malaysian Navy's (RMN) Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) projects, despite the ongoing global energy crisis. Its deputy minister, Adly Zahari said operational readiness and national security would not be compromised. Any aspect involving national security, sovereignty, and future planning will proceed as scheduled," he said.

items.title: Penang plans new state administrative centre, legislative assembly on Silicon Island
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 7 — The Penang government is planning the construction of a new state administrative centre including a new state legislative assembly on Silicon Island. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the Penang Development Corporation (PDC) was appointed to assess spatial and design requirements from various state agencies. He said data collection is underway to enable detailed architectural and land use planning for the administrative centre.

items.title: Johor offers full scholarships for SPM top scorers to world's top 20 universities [WATCH]
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor government has opened applications for its flagship youth scholarship programme offering full sponsorship for top-performing SPM students to pursue degrees at the world's leading universities. State Unity, Heritage and Culture Committee chairman K. Raven Kumar said the Johor Orang Muda Scholarship (BOMJ) 2026, administered by Yayasan Pelajaran Johor, aims to nurture a new generation of global talent from the state. Raven said priority would be given to applicants pursuing fields aligned with Johor's strategic development needs, to ensure graduates contribute to the state's future growth.

items.title: Former Johor property executive pleads not guilty to RM1m CBT charges
items.summary: Rafidah Abdul Hamid, 48, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read out before Judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. According to the charge sheet, the accused is charged with committing criminal breach of trust by fraudulently using money, amounting to between RM73,000 and RM317,610, for her own benefit. while the accused unrepresented.

items.title: Land for Silicon Island LRT depot set for handover in May, says Penang CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 7 — Land for the Light Rail Transit (LRT) depot on Silicon Island is expected to be handed over to MRT Corporation Sdn Bhd (MRTC) as early as May this year, said Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. Chow said Silicon Island represents more than just a land reclamation project but a long-term commitment by the Penang government to future generations. An artist’s impression of Penang Silicon Island.

items.title: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson resigns amid losses and regulatory scrutiny, source says
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Air India chief executive officer (CEO) Campbell Wilson has resigned, a source with direct knowledge of the matter said on Tuesday, as the airline grapples with persistent losses and heightened regulatory scrutiny following a crash last year that killed 260 people. Air India did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment outside regular business hours. Air India and its low-cost carrier Air India Express reported a combined loss of 98.08 billion rupees (US$1.05 billion) in the 2024-2025 financial year.

items.title: Four siblings draw attention with unique names inspired by brands, icons
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Four siblings from Gombak near here are drawing attention for their distinctive names inspired by luxury brands, natural phenomena and global sports icons. The third of the four, Hajarr Ferarie Mohd Zaki, 20, said she and her siblings – M'Glazzia Zidannial, 25, Shayenn Qistina, 22, and Emell Lebronz, 19 – were each given unconventional names based on their father's creative ideas. Hajarr Ferarie said each name carries its own meaning.

items.title: Suspected Malayan tiger footprints found in Kluang, Perhilitan steps up patrols
items.summary: KLUANG: Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) officers were placed on high alert after suspected tiger paw prints were discovered at a modern agricultural project here. The prints raised concerns over the presence of the endangered predator beyond its usual forest range. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said the department received a report on the discovery at 7.30pm on April 3.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s fuel supply secure until June, thanks Iranian president for safe transit of ships via Hormuz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Malaysia has sufficient fuel supply to meet local consumption needs until June, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim reiterated today. Speaking at the Kuala Lumpur-Ankara Dialogue 2026, Anwar credited national oil giant Petronas for building robust ties with other oil producing nations that has ensured adequate fuel supply for Malaysia despite the ongoing war in West Asia. Anwar also expressed his gratitude to Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian for promising the safe passage of Malaysian ships carrying crude oil across the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: UPSI bowling centre employee found dead had complained of headache, says manager
items.summary: Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) Sports Bowl manager Dzulkarnain Sapian said the employee was only found around 1am after his wife came to the bowling centre to look for her husband, who had not returned home after finishing work last night. “The victim had complained to his wife last evening about having a headache. Dzulkarnain said the victim, Mohd Radzi Ariffin, 53, was a cleaner at the private company, UPSI Sports Bowl, which operates within the UPSI campus.

items.title: Anwar: Fuel prices may rise, but Malaysia unlikely to face oil crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is unlikely to face an oil supply crisis in the coming months despite global uncertainties, although fuel prices may rise slightly. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said this is due to Petronas' strong track record as a net oil importer and the company's excellent relationships with major oil-producing countries. "Praise must be given to Petronas for their excellent performance and good relations with oil-producing countries.

items.title: Trailer driver charged with murder over Johor crash that killed three family members
items.summary: SEGAMAT, April 7 — The trailer driver involved in the fatal crash that claimed the lives of three family members at KM212 of the Johor Bahru–Seremban Road, near the Taman Golden Hill junction in Gemas Baharu last week, was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today. The accused, Muhamad Shafiq Salleh, 29, nodded in understanding as the charge was read before Magistrate Amalina Johar. Earlier, Transport Minister Anthony Loke directed the Road Transport Department (JPJ) and the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) to conduct an immediate audit and investigation into the trailer operator.

items.title: Ekuinas takes strategic stake in Ain Medicare via RM1bil fund
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ekuiti Nasional Bhd (Ekuinas) has made a strategic investment in Ain Medicare Sdn Bhd, a Bumiputera-owned manufacturer of sterile parenteral pharmaceutical products. The investment, made through Ekuinas' RM1 billion Tranche IV fund, will see the fund acquire a minority stake in Ain Medicare. Ekuinas said the move supports its mandate to grow Bumiputera enterprises in strategic sectors that enhance Malaysia's long-term economic resilience.

items.title: East Coast tour bus operators face shutdown over diesel price hike
items.summary: KOTA BARU: About 1,000 tour bus operators on the East Coast not in the list of diesel subsidy recipients under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) risk being shut down following the rise in diesel prices. Deputy chairman of the East Coast Bus Association (Pebat), Wan Mohd Nuhairi Wan Hamat, said tour bus operators do not fall under the category of companies eligible for SKDS, as the subsidy is limited to larger tourism firms. He said operators have been forced to absorb higher diesel costs to continue operations.

items.title: MOE takes responsibility for kayaking incident that killed 2 trainee teachers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek says the Education Ministry (MOE) will take full responsibility for the kayaking incident that claimed the lives of two trainee teachers during an off-campus programme. Fadhlina visited Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital in Alor Star to pay her last respects to the victims, identified as Christopher Ling Jia Siang and Joey Ling Lin Siang. "The ministry takes full responsibility for the accident.

items.title: Anwar backs Turkiye’s call for restraint in West Asia, praises Egypt, Oman and Pakistan mediating US-Iran tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said Malaysia fully backs Turkiye’s advocacy for all parties to exercise restraint in the West Asia war that erupted over a month ago. Anwar said international law should be advocated and applied consistently in order for a rules-based order to have credibility. “It cannot protect some while denying others their legitimate rights,” Anwar said at the inaugural Kuala Lumpur-Ankara Dialogue 2026 at the Grand Hyatt Kuala Lumpur, here, today.

items.title: Anwar: Global supply risks force urgent energy strategy overhaul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must urgently shift to a more sustainable, diversified and cost-effective energy strategy to safeguard long-term security, as global supply disruptions pose growing risks. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move is not merely an environmental measure, but a critical strategic step to ensure national resilience. "In the immediate term, the government has acted to manage exposure to volatility, balancing supply needs with the imperative of mitigating cost-of-living pressures," he said.

items.title: Tugboats caught operating illegally in Johor waters
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Two tugboats attempting to slip into Malaysian waters without authorisation were detained by the Johor Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) Johor in separate operations off the state's east coast on Sunday. Acting Johor MMEA director Maritime Captain Kama Azri Kamil said the arrests were made during coordinated patrols under multiple operations. Both vessels were found operating within Malaysian territorial waters, placing them under the jurisdiction of local maritime enforcement authorities.

items.title: Authorities struggle as crypto drives new wave of money laundering
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Rapid advances in digital technology are opening new avenues for financial crime, with illicit funds increasingly channelled through cryptocurrencies. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said cryptocurrency transactions, conducted entirely in the virtual space, require a different approach to detection compared with conventional financial transactions. The National Financial Crime Centre (NFCC) is also facing challenges in tracking money laundering activities involving cryptocurrencies.

items.title: Singaporean professionals fined RM1,000, ordered community service for littering cigarette butts outside Johor mall
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — Two Singaporean professionals were fined RM1,000 each and ordered to perform six hours of community service by the Sessions Court here today for throwing cigarette butts in a public place. Lim, an architect, and Tan, a project manager, were ordered to complete six hours of community service at a rate of two hours per day within a period not exceeding six months. The Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act 2007 came into force nationwide on January 1.

items.title: [UPDATED] Customs seizes 35.6 tonnes of dried shark fins worth RM8.24 million
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Customs Department has seized 35.6 tonnes of dried shark fins worth RM8.24 million, believed to have been imported without valid permits and intended for sale to high-end restaurants in the country. Selangor Customs director Mohamad Azhar Ahmad Paharaz said the seizure involved three containers intercepted at the North Port Free Zone in Port Klang during operations conducted on Feb 12, March 2 and March 9 under Ops Petasan. "The containers were carrying dried shark fins weighing 35,660kg, believed to have been imported without valid permits.

items.title: 'I didn't understand': Cambodian man shocked after US deportation, jailed in Eswatini
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: A Cambodian refugee long-settled in the United States, ex-convict Pheap Rom, remains bewildered at how he wound up behind bars in the African nation of Eswatini for months after being swept up in Donald Trump's deportation blitz. Rom is one of around 20 men the United States has deported to landlocked Eswatini – bordering South Africa and Mozambique – under a Trump administration scheme challenged in courts and described by rights advocates as akin to "human trafficking." Eswatini, Africa's last absolute monarchy, is among several "third countries" accepting migrants under shadowy deals enabling the US president's push for mass deportations.

items.title: Trailer driver charged with three counts of murder after Johor crash [WATCH]
items.summary: SEGAMAT: A trailer driver was charged in the magistrate's court today with three counts of murder over a collision that killed three family members on April 2. Muhamad Shafiq Salleh, 29, nodded to indicate he understood the charges read out by magistrate Amalina Johar. The three family members died in a crash involving three vehicles near the Taman Golden Hill junction in Gemas Baru at 4pm last Thursday.

items.title: Stubbed out: Two Singaporeans fined RM1,000 each for littering cigarette butts in JB
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU: Two Singaporean men were fined RM1,000 each and ordered to perform community service by the Sessions Court today for littering in a public place. Che Wan Zaidi also ordered the two to perform six hours of community service, to be completed within six months. The charge was framed under Section 77A(1) of the Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act 2007 (Act 672), which allows for community service orders under Subsection 77B(1) of the same Act.

items.title: Seremban Jaya assemblyman’s childhood home goes up in flames, family safe in dinner outing
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 7 — The family home of Seremban Jaya assemblyman Datuk P. Gunasekaran was destroyed in a fire at Kampung Baru Rahang here last night. Gunasekaran said his late parents and three siblings used to live in the house, bought around 1990. “This old house holds many memories.

items.title: Ethical dilemma of AI warfare without human oversight
items.summary: AI systems process vast streams of battlefield data, from satellite imagery to drone feeds, and convert them into actionable decisions almost instantly. In 2006, the Israeli military identified about 300 targets in Lebanon. By 2023, AI-enabled systems generated around 15,000 targets within just 35 days of operations in Gaza.

items.title: Fadhlina vows thorough probe, accountability after IPG kayaking fatalities in Perlis
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — The Ministry of Education (MoE) is reviewing the standard operating procedures (SOPs) for outdoor activities across all its institutions to strengthen safety measures. The review comes after an accident at Pantai Merdeka last Saturday, which claimed the lives of two trainee teachers from the Institute of Teacher Education (IPG) Perlis Campus during an off-campus programme. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the ministry takes full responsibility for the tragedy.

items.title: 4 policemen remanded in RM12,000 bribery probe by MACC [WATCH]
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Four policemen were remanded for 5 days by the magistrate's court here today over a RM12,000 bribery case that took place last year. The remand, issued by magistrate Rais Imran Hamid, runs until April 11. The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) obtained the remand under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code.

items.title: Cops arrest 2 men over alleged assault of woman in Penang
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: Police have arrested two men over criminal intimidation, mischief, and causing hurt after a woman was injured in an incident on Jalan Tun Sardon on March 4. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the investigation is being conducted under Sections 323, 506, and 427 of the Penal Code. The incident left a 40-year-old woman injured.

items.title: CIMB targets Asean wealth boom under Forward30 plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd is ramping up its push into Asean's fast-growing affluent and wealth segment. The banking group is targeting a rapidly expanding market, with Asean's affluent population expected to grow 5.0-6.0 per cent annually. To support this, CIMB has invested heavily in enhancing its wealth capabilities.

items.title: Police refer Balik Pulau assault case to DPP after outcry over no charges
items.summary: Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the investigation paper is being prepared for submission. “The case is being investigated under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt, Section 506 for criminal intimidation and Section 427 for mischief,” he said in a statement issued today. “The victim received treatment at Balik Pulau Hospital,” he said.

items.title: Sunway, IJM mixed in early trade after takeover lapse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Bhd and IJM Corp Bhd show mixed early trade moves on Tuesday, following the lapse of the former's takeover offer. At the time of writing, Sunway shares were up one sen or 0.20 per cent to RM5.00, giving it a market capitalisation of RM34.03 billion, while IJM shares were down seven sen or 2.97 per cent to RM2.29, with a market capitalisation of RM8.35 billion. Trading was moderate, with IJM changing hands about 2.00 million shares and Sunway about 1.02 million shares.

items.title: Second Filipino killed in Middle East war after missile hits home in Israel
items.summary: MANILA: The Middle East war has claimed its second Philippine victim, Manila said Tuesday, when a missile struck the home of a Filipina living in Israel. The woman was killed in the port city of Haifa on Sunday "alongside her Israeli husband and elderly parents-in-law", the foreign affairs department said, without naming the victims. Israeli rescue services said Monday that the bodies of four people had been recovered from the rubble of a residential building in the city, after it was struck by an Iranian missile the previous day.

items.title: UPSI Sports Bowl staff member found dead in locked storage room in Tanjong Malim
items.summary: IPOH, April 7 — A staff member at the Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) Sports Bowl in Tanjong Malim was found dead in the bowling centre’s cleaning equipment storage room early this morning. “The male victim, 53-year-old Mohd Radzi Ariffin, was trapped in a locked storage room. UPSI auxiliary police opened the door upon the firefighters’ arrival.

items.title: Taiwan opposition leader makes rare visit to China
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwan's main opposition leader will travel to China on Tuesday for a rare visit aimed at building cross-strait "peace", but the government warns Beijing will seek to stop US arms sales to the democratic island. Kuomintang (KMT) chairwoman Cheng Li-wun, who will become the party's first leader to visit China in a decade, has insisted on meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping before she visits the United States – Taiwan's main security backer. "To preserve peace is to preserve Taiwan," Cheng told a press conference at KMT headquarters in Taipei.

items.title: Global uncertainties test resilience of Malaysia's halal industry, says Zahid
items.summary: TASEK GELUGOR: Global uncertainties are increasingly shaping the trajectory of the halal industry, with geopolitical developments now exerting direct pressure on supply chains and production costs. "These disruptions have significantly impacted global supply chains," he said, noting that the effects extend beyond the energy sector and into the broader halal ecosystem. The national halal food sector holds significant growth potential, with the global halal market currently valued at over RM16.02 trillion and projected to expand to RM22.88 trillion by 2030.

items.title: Vietnam parliament elects party chief To Lam as president
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnam Communist Party boss To Lam was elected president by the National Assembly on Tuesday, capping his bid to centralise authority in a nation where senior cadres have traditionally governed collectively. After securing another term as the party's general secretary in January, Lam will now take over the number two position in Vietnamese politics – unifying leadership of the party and state as President Xi Jinping did in neighbouring China. Tran Thanh Man, chairman of the National Assembly, said 100 per cent of deputies who were present approved the resolution electing Lam president for the 2026-2031 term.

items.title: In Kelantan, four cops remanded five days until April 11 over RM12,000 bribery case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Four police personnel were remanded for five days by the Kota Bharu Magistrates’ Court today to assist in an investigation into a RM12,000 bribery case last year. The remand order, which runs until April 11, was issued by Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid, reported BuletinTV3 today. The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) applied for the remand under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code.

items.title: Up in flames: Chery Tiggo SUV 60pc destroyed in five-vehicle crash in Beruas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A Chery Tiggo sports utility vehicle (SUV) was about 60 per cent destroyed in a five-vehicle crash at Changkat Cermin, Ayer Tawar near Beruas in Perak on Monday. The Ayer Tawar Fire and Rescue Station received an emergency call about the incident involving five people at 4.55pm, according to Sinar Harian . Perak Deputy Operations Director of the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM), Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad, said the crash involved a Proton X50, Proton Inspira, the Chery Tiggo, and two Proton Wiras.

items.title: No-go for ‘rempits’: KB airport clamps down on rogue racers, cones and patrols now in place (VIDEO)
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 7 — Malaysia Airports has clamped down on illegal street racing at the Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP). In a statement today, Malaysia Airports said that it has taken proactive measures in collaboration with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and Road Transport Department (JPJ) to strengthen enforcement and prevent such activities. “This measure serves as an extra precaution to prevent irresponsible individuals from using the area for illegal street racing,” the statement added.

items.title: Perlis kayaking deaths: Families of drowned trainee teachers seek accountability
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 7 — The family of a trainee teacher from the Perlis Campus of the Institute of Teacher Education (IPG), who drowned while kayaking at Pantai Merdeka near Sungai Petani last Saturday, has expressed hope that such a tragedy will never happen again. Joey Ling Lin Siang’s aunt, Kee Ching Ching, 46, said the family is also calling for a full and transparent report into the incident by the relevant authorities. Last Saturday, Bachelor of Teaching (PISMP) Physical Education students Christopher Ling Jia Siang, 21, and Joey, 20, were found drowned at about 7.30 pm while kayaking at Pantai Merdeka near Sungai Petani.

items.title: Daim’s daughter pleads not guilty to failing to declare company shareholdings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The daughter of the late former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin was charged in the Sessions Court here today with failing to declare her shareholdings in five companies in 2023. Asnida Abdul Daim, 65, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read out to her before Judge Rosli Ahmad. The shares involved five companies: Amiraz Sdn Bhd, Arida Farms Sdn Bhd, Kangkung Catering Sdn Bhd, Transgrade Sdn Bhd, and Gajah3 Foods Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Heavy rain and gusty winds for Selangor, Penang, Johor, Perlis, and Kedah till noon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to affect five states in Peninsular Malaysia until noon today, said the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a statement, MetMalaysia said the conditions are forecast in Perlis and Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda and Kulim), as well as Penang (Seberang Perai Utara and Seberang Perai Tengah). The same warning has been issued for Selangor (Klang and Kuala Langat) and Johor (Tangkak, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai and Johor Bahru).

items.title: Five-year cap for contract pharmacists, permanent posts conditional, Health Ministry clarifies
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has clarified that there will be no contract extensions for pharmacists whose services are reportedly ending, as they have reached the maximum contract tenure of five years. The ministry said it has taken note of concerns raised in a media report titled “45 Contract Pharmacists in Limbo as Jobs End Saturday” published on April 2. MOH also explained that any offer of permanent appointment is conditional.

items.title: Sunset cruise off Kedah hits choppy waters with MMEA over too many passengers on board
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 7 — A sunset cruise vessel was detained by the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) for carrying passengers beyond the allowed limit Sunday. Kedah and Perlis MMEA director Romli Mustafa said the vessel was detained at about 5.20 pm by an MMEA patrol boat during a routine patrol 0.3 nautical miles east of Pulau Bumbon Besar. He said inspections found that the vessel had committed an offence under the Merchant Shipping Ordinance 1952 for carrying passengers exceeding the permitted capacity.

items.title: Greenpeace joins Malaysia-led flotilla to challenge Gaza siege, over 100 vessels expected
items.summary: OVIEDO (Spain), April — Greenpeace has announced that it will join the upcoming humanitarian Global Sumud Flotilla to Gaza – an effort to break the years-long blockade of the Palestinian enclave – contributing a vessel to provide technical and operational support, Anadolu Ajansi reported. “Greenpeace’s history of defending the seas, confronting injustice and taking action in defence of life makes them a powerful addition to our 2026 spring mission,” said Susan Abdullah, member of the Global Sumud Flotilla Steering Committee, in a statement. Last September, the flotilla set sail from Spain and eventually included 43 boats and 462 people.

items.title: Fatal right turn: Motorcyclist killed in crash with car in Cyberjaya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A motorcyclist in his 20s was killed in a collision with a car along Jalan Persiaran Semarak Api in Cyberjaya yesterday morning. Sepang police chief Assistant Commissioner Norhizam Bahaman said the crash occurred at about 8.20am, the New Straits Times reported. “The motorcyclist was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel from Hospital Cyberjaya,” Norhizam said in a statement.

items.title: Daim’s daughter Asnida arrives at court in KL over alleged failure to declare assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The daughter of former Finance Minister, the late Tun Daim Zainuddin, arrived at the Kuala Lumpur Court Complex to face charges of failing to comply with a notice issued by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to declare her assets. Asnida Abdul Daim arrived at the court complex at 8.40 am, accompanied by her family members. Yesterday, Bernama reported that lawyer Yu Ying Ying, who is representing Asnida, said the MACC had informed her client that she would be charged under Section 36(2) of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Police seize RM55m in scrap metal and e-waste crackdown, arrest over 200 people
items.summary: KLANG, April 7 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), through the Internal Security and Public Order Department (JKDNKA), today launched Op Hazard 3.0, aimed at dismantling illegal premises and illicit e-waste management operators nationwide. Bukit Aman JKDNKA General Operations Force (GOF) deputy director Datuk Mohamad Suzrin Mohamad Rodhi said that as of 6 pm, more than 110 premises had been raided nationwide, including e-waste processing factories, scrap metal yards, and illegal premises. Mohamad Suzrin said the operation also led to the arrest of 205 individuals, comprising 39 locals, 66 legal foreign nationals, and 100 undocumented migrants.

items.title: Toman fishing trip turns deadly: Man drowns after boat capsizes in Kedah river
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A fishing outing ended in tragedy when a man drowned while his cousin survived after their boat capsized in Sungai Kubang Rotan, Kuala Kedah, yesterday afternoon. The victim, Muhammad Zulkifli Ahmad Tarmizi, 24, from Kuala Sungai, was found drowned by fire and rescue personnel, Harian Metro reported today. Kota Setar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali said authorities received a report at 4.28pm on the incident.

items.title: Communications minister settles defamation suit against Papagomo over Rawang mosque speech
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The 2023 defamation suit filed by Datuk Fahmi Fadzil against Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, or ‘Papagomo’, over allegations that the Communications Minister gave a political speech at a mosque in Rawang, Selangor, was settled in the High Court here yesterday. Fahmi’s lawyer, Asheeq Ali Sethi Alivi, said at a press conference that the case had been resolved amicably. Meanwhile, Asheeq said the defamation suit against independent preacher Ahmad Dusuki Abd Rani will proceed to trial tomorrow, after the parties failed to settle, with Fahmi set to begin giving his testimony.

items.title: Lorry driver who tested positive for drugs to face murder charge over Segamat crash that killed three
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A lorry driver who tested positive for drugs and crashed into the rear of a van carrying a family of five, killing three of them, will be charged with murder at the Magistrates’ Court in Segamat, Johor, today. Segamat district police chief Superintendent Mohd Jumazanzahir Chek Ismail said the 29-year-old suspect will be charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code, according to a report in Berita Harian today. He said police received instructions from the Johor State Prosecution Office to charge the suspect under Section 302 instead of Section 44(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Fuziah: PKR mulls disciplinary move as pressure mounts over Rafizi’s criticisms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — PKR may refer calls for action against its former deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli to the party’s disciplinary board, secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh said. Fuziah said the matter would be discussed internally following mounting pressure from party leaders, the New Straits Times reported. A day later, Melaka PKR deputy chairman Mohd Khuzaire Mohd Kamal warned that Rafizi’s remarks could undermine unity within the party and erode confidence in its president, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

items.title: Asnida Daim to face charge in KL court today as MACC widens ‘Op Godfather’ crackdown
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — A daughter of former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin will be charged at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court today. Lawyer Yu Ying Ying confirmed that Asnida Daim will be charged under Section 36(2) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009. However, Tun Daim’s three other children, namely Md Wira Dani, Muhammed Amir Zainuddin and Muhammed Amin Zainuddin, are understood to have yet to arrive at the MACC Headquarters.
